# Literature Review Map

## Manufactured attention and coordination

Use Rogers & Righetti as the bridge from political CIB into commercial attention manufacture.

- **Rogers & Righetti (2025) — Coordinated inauthentic behaviour on Facebook? A typology of manufactured attention** (Academic / open-access). Defines manufactured attention as artificial audience fabrication through coordinated behaviour; surfaces advertising networks, gambling/cyber-scam promotion, hijacked public groups, media groups, and political/activist coordination. URL: https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/29768624251369784

## Recommender systems

Use recommender systems research to explain why multi-signal manipulation matters.

- **A comprehensive survey on multi-behavior recommender systems (2026)** (Academic / open-access). Shows modern recommender systems learn from many interaction types such as clicks, carts, purchases and ratings; useful for explaining why proxy-signal manipulation is powerful. URL: https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s41060-025-01019-z

## Reviews and testimonials

Use FTC, ACCC, Trustpilot, Amazon, and fake-review research to show credibility-signal corruption.

- **FTC final rule banning fake reviews and testimonials (2024)** (Regulator / primary). Prohibits fake/false reviews and testimonials, buying reviews conditioned on sentiment, review suppression, insider reviews without disclosure, and misuse of fake social-media indicators. URL: https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2024/08/federal-trade-commission-announces-final-rule-banning-fake-reviews-testimonials
- **ACCC — PhotobookShop pays penalties for influencer reviews (2026)** (Regulator / primary). PhotobookShop paid AU$39,600 in penalties after allegedly instructing influencers not to disclose gifted products on 107 occasions and editing negative review comments out of a reposted influencer review. URL: https://www.accc.gov.au/media-release/photobookshop-pays-penalties-for-influencer-reviews
- **Trustpilot Trust Report 2025** (Platform transparency / primary). Trustpilot reported 7.4% of submitted reviews detected and removed as fake in 2024; reported 3.7 million fabricated reviews removed automatically and 19k warnings issued. URL: https://corporate.trustpilot.com/trust/trust-report-2025
- **Amazon continues legal actions to protect customers from fake reviews** (Company enforcement / primary). Amazon reported legal actions against fake review brokers and said nearly 350,000 people using broker websites were no longer incentivized to write fake/misleading Amazon reviews. URL: https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/policy-news-views/amazon-continues-legal-actions-to-protect-customers-from-fake-reviews

## Dark patterns and consumer protection

Use OECD and FTC sweep evidence to show post-attention manipulation.

- **OECD — Dark commercial patterns** (Policy / intergovernmental). Dark commercial patterns steer, deceive, coerce, or manipulate consumers into decisions not in their best interest; exploit biases to extract money, data or attention. URL: https://www.oecd.org/en/topics/sub-issues/dark-commercial-patterns.html
- **FTC/ICPEN/GPEN dark patterns sweep results** (Regulator / primary). Review of 642 subscription websites/apps found nearly 76% used at least one possible dark pattern and nearly 67% used multiple possible dark patterns. URL: https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2024/07/ftc-icpen-gpen-announce-results-review-use-dark-patterns-affecting-subscription-services-privacy
